fields.label: Sustain Requirement Timing
fields.why: Some spells require a later upkeep action after the initial casting; that timing is mechanically different from an immediate trigger.
fields.comment: Records when a spell's upkeep action must be paid. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Timing keeps later-turn maintenance costs out of prose-only descriptions. Populate it from canonical text that says the caster, target, or another actor must spend a later action to maintain a spell or effect. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.timing`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no sustain requirement.

acceptedValueNotes.later_turns: Use when the upkeep action applies on turns after the initial casting turn.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when canonical prose has no action-based sustain requirement.

log.why: Added for Crown of Madness requiring later Magic actions to maintain control.

fields.label: Sustain Requirement Actor
fields.why: The runtime needs to know who must pay the upkeep cost.
fields.comment: Names the actor who must pay a spell's upkeep action. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Actor keeps upkeep responsibility explicit instead of hiding it in the description. Populate it from canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.actor`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no sustain requirement.

acceptedValueNotes.caster: Use when the spellcaster must spend the sustain action.
acceptedValueNotes.target: Use when the original spell target must spend the sustain action.
acceptedValueNotes.affected_creature: Use when any currently affected creature may be the sustain actor.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no sustain actor applies.

log.why: Added for Crown of Madness requiring the caster to maintain control.

fields.label: Sustain Requirement Action Type
fields.why: Canonical prose can name a rules-facing action category, such as Magic action, that is more specific than the action-economy slot.
fields.comment: Records the rules-facing action category required for upkeep. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Action Type preserves wording such as Magic action instead of flattening it to only `action`. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.actionKind`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no sustain requirement or the prose gives no special action category.

acceptedValueNotes.magic_action: Use when canonical prose specifically requires the Magic action.
acceptedValueNotes.standard_action: Use when canonical prose requires an ordinary action without the Magic-action tag.
acceptedValueNotes.bonus_action: Use when canonical prose requires a Bonus Action.
acceptedValueNotes.reaction: Use when canonical prose requires a Reaction.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no sustain action category applies.

log.why: Added so Crown of Madness can preserve Magic action rather than only generic action cost.

fields.label: Sustain Requirement Action Cost
fields.why: The action-economy cost must be available separately from the rules-facing action category.
fields.comment: Records the action-economy slot consumed by the upkeep action. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Action Cost lets runtime action-budget checks read the cost directly while Action Type preserves the canonical category. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.actionCost`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no sustain requirement.

acceptedValueNotes.action: Use when the sustain requirement consumes the actor's action.
acceptedValueNotes.bonus_action: Use when the sustain requirement consumes the actor's Bonus Action.
acceptedValueNotes.reaction: Use when the sustain requirement consumes the actor's Reaction.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no action-economy sustain cost applies.

log.why: Added for Crown of Madness's action-costed maintenance.

fields.label: Sustain Requirement Failure Outcome
fields.why: The runtime needs the consequence when the required upkeep action is not paid.
fields.comment: Records what happens when a sustain requirement is missed. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Failure Outcome keeps upkeep failure explicit and pairs with Conditional Ending Triggers when failure ends the spell or effect. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.failureOutcome`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no sustain requirement.

acceptedValueNotes.spell_ends: Use when failure to pay the upkeep action ends the whole spell.
acceptedValueNotes.effect_ends: Use when failure to pay the upkeep action ends only one effect packet.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no sustain failure consequence applies.

log.why: Added for Crown of Madness ending when the caster does not maintain control.
